source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 调校 (机器 、 系统等 ); HG improve (a mechanism or system) by making fine adjustments to it: Our software developers are tweaking the program. 我们的软件工程师正在

headline.core_en: to make small precise changes in order to improve something

familiar_bridge.summary: fine-tune 是“细调”；tweak 也是小幅调整，常用于程序、设计、设置或计划。

core.main: tweak 不表示大改，而是对已经存在的东西做一点点调整，让它更顺、更准或更有效。

usage_groups.group_title: 对程序、设计或方案做小幅调整
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 微调；稍作改动

examples.sentence: Our software developers are tweaking the program.
examples.translation: 我们的软件工程师正在微调这个程序。
examples.teaching_note: 原书例句；tweak 和 program 搭配，表示小幅技术调整。

examples.sentence: The researchers tweaked the model to better fit the new data.
examples.translation: 研究人员对模型做了微调，使它更符合新数据。
examples.teaching_note: 学术语境中常用于模型、方法或参数的细调。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She adjusted the camera before taking the photo.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: adjust 更普通；tweak 更强调小而精细的改动。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The editor changed the title of the article.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: change 范围很大；tweak 通常只是小改。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Engineers fine-tuned the engine for better performance.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: fine-tune 与 tweak 很接近，常用于技术或系统优化。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The team modified the design after the test.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: modify 更正式，改动可大可小。

antonyms.example_sentence: The company overhauled its entire training system.
antonyms.difference_note: overhaul 是大规模重做；tweak 是小幅微调。

antonyms.example_sentence: The committee left the original wording unchanged.

word_family.example_sentence: The tweaked design loaded faster on older phones.
word_family.difference_note: 过去分词作形容词，表示已经做过小改。

word_family.example_sentence: The app has a tweakable interface for advanced users.
word_family.difference_note: 表示可以被细调，适合识别词形。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 熟练的; 擅长的; 精通的 competent or skilled in doing or using something: / was proficient at my job. 我擅长我的工作 。 还区词 adept

headline.core_en: skilled and competent at doing or using something

familiar_bridge.summary: proficient 比 good at 更正式，强调已经有稳定熟练度。

core.main: proficient 常形容人在语言、技能、工具或工作上达到熟练水平。

usage_groups.group_title: 熟练掌握某种技能、语言或工具
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调能力可靠、做得熟练。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 教育、工作、技能评估和简历语境中常见。

examples.sentence: She became proficient in French after two years of study.
examples.translation: 她学习两年后熟练掌握了法语。
examples.teaching_note: proficient in 常接语言或领域。

examples.sentence: A proficient technician can identify the problem within minutes.
examples.translation: 熟练的技术员几分钟内就能找出问题。
examples.teaching_note: 可直接修饰掌握技能的人。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Skilled workers repaired the machine quickly.
The new assistant is competent but still learning.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: competent 强调能胜任，不一定很出色。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She is adept at explaining complex ideas simply.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: adept 常接 at，语气较正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He is an accomplished pianist.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: accomplished 强调成就和成熟技巧。

antonyms.example_sentence: Inexperienced drivers often hesitate in heavy traffic.
The inept repair made the problem worse.

word_family.example_sentence: The exam measures proficiency in academic English.
She handled the software proficiently.

related_words.example_sentence: An efficient worker wastes little time.
related_words.difference_note: 拼写相近但含义不同：efficient 强调效率。

source.raw_ocr_text: #ka EL n. (对待基人的 ) 方式 manner of treating: il! usage at the hands of his jailors 4k 3 JE 4%

headline.core_en: the way something is used, especially a word or expression; also a manner of treatment

familiar_bridge.summary: usage 来自 use，但更像“使用方式/惯用方式”；旧式表达里也可指对人的对待。

core.main: usage 最常见是语言或物品的使用方式；原书还收了较正式、较旧的“对待方式”。

usage_groups.group_title: 词语、表达或物品的使用方式
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 表示某个词、结构或东西如何被使用。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 语言学习、写作、说明书和学术讨论中常见。

examples.sentence: Modern usage allows the word to appear as both a noun and a verb.
examples.translation: 现代用法允许这个词既作名词也作动词。
examples.teaching_note: usage 在语言学习中非常常见。

examples.sentence: The manual explains the proper usage of the device.
examples.translation: 说明书解释了该设备的正确使用方法。
examples.teaching_note: 也可指物品的使用方式。

usage_groups.group_title: 某人受到的对待方式
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 较正式或旧式地表示待遇、对待。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 文学或历史语境中较常见。
common_patterns: usage at the hands of someone

examples.sentence: The prisoner complained of harsh usage at the hands of his guards.
examples.translation: 囚犯抱怨自己受到看守的粗暴对待。
examples.teaching_note: 这个义项较书面，核心是 treatment。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The use of the term changed over time.
The practice became common in schools.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Academic convention requires careful citation.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: convention 更强调约定俗成。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report criticized the treatment of prisoners.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 在“对待方式”义上接近。

antonyms.example_sentence: The misuse of statistics weakened the argument.
antonyms.difference_note: misuse 表示错误或不当使用。

word_family.example_sentence: The tool is easy to use.
The data were incomplete but still usable.
The guide is useful for beginners.

related_words.example_sentence: Local custom shaped the ceremony.
related_words.difference_note: 和 usage 的“惯用方式”有关。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 显示; 展示; 表明 display or show (a quality or feeling) by one's acts or appearance; demonstrate: He manifested signs of severe depression. 他表现出了严重的抑郁症状 。 i 34] reveal adj. 显然的; 明显的 easily understood or recognized by the mind; obvious: manifest superiority 明显的优势 | It was manifest to all of us that he would fail. 他要失败 ， 这一点对我们大家来说是显而易见的 。 i354 self-evident

headline.core_en: to show a feeling or quality clearly; obvious and easy to recognize

familiar_bridge.summary: manifest 可以是动词“显现出来”，也可以是形容词“明显的”。

core.main: manifest 的核心是某种特征、情绪或事实变得可见、可辨认。

usage_groups.group_title: 情绪、特征或问题显现出来
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 表示内在状态通过行为、症状或结果表现出来。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 心理、医学、社会分析和正式写作中常见。

examples.sentence: The disease may manifest itself first as a mild fever.
examples.translation: 这种疾病最初可能表现为轻微发烧。
examples.teaching_note: manifest itself as/in 表示以某种形式显现。

examples.sentence: His impatience manifested itself in short, sharp replies.
examples.translation: 他的不耐烦体现在简短而尖锐的回答中。
examples.teaching_note: 抽象情绪可以通过行为表现出来。

usage_groups.group_title: 某事显而易见、清楚可见
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 形容事实、优势或意图明显到容易识别。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 正式评论和论证中常见。

examples.sentence: It was manifest that the plan could not succeed without public support.
examples.translation: 很明显，没有公众支持，这个计划不可能成功。
examples.teaching_note: 形容词 manifest 表示 obvious。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The data show a clear trend.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: show 更日常，语气更普通。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The mistake was obvious to everyone.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 对应 manifest 作形容词时的“明显”。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The experiment revealed a hidden pattern.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: reveal 强调把隐藏内容显露出来。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her disappointment was evident in her voice.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: evident 与 manifest 的形容词义接近。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report concealed important evidence.
The jargon obscured the main point.

word_family.example_sentence: The protest was a manifestation of public anger.
The accusation was manifestly false.

related_words.example_sentence: The evidence made the conclusion clear.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. (法律等 ) 极为严厉的; 严酷的 (of laws or their application) excessively harsh and severe: draconian forms of punishment 残酷的惩罚形式

headline.core_en: excessively harsh or severe, especially of laws, rules, or punishments

familiar_bridge.summary: draconian 常让人想到“法律太狠”：不是普通 strict，而是严到过分。

core.main: draconian 常批评法律、政策、惩罚或措施过度严厉。

usage_groups.group_title: 法律、政策或惩罚严厉到过分
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调处罚或规则严重超出合理范围。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 政治、法律、政策评论中常见。

examples.sentence: The new law imposed draconian penalties for minor offenses.
examples.translation: 这项新法律对轻微违法行为施加了严酷惩罚。
examples.teaching_note: draconian penalties 强调惩罚过重。

examples.sentence: Critics called the restrictions draconian and unnecessary.
examples.translation: 批评者称这些限制严苛且没有必要。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于批评政策或措施。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The punishment seemed harsh for such a small mistake.
The school adopted severe rules.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The regime imposed oppressive controls on speech.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: oppressive 强调压制自由。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The tax was seen as punitive by small businesses.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: punitive 强调以惩罚为目的。

antonyms.example_sentence: The judge was lenient with first-time offenders.
A permissive policy allowed broad experimentation.

word_family.example_sentence: Draco is remembered for extremely severe laws.
word_family.difference_note: 词源提示，帮助理解“严酷法律”。

related_words.example_sentence: The spelling may remind students of dragon, but draconian comes from Draco.
related_words.difference_note: 只作记忆提醒，不当作派生词。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 世界性的; 全球的 familiar with and at ease in many different countries and cultures: The population of New York is very cosmopolitan. 纽约的居民来自世界各地 。 | Music is one of the most cosmopolitan of the arts. 音乐是最具有世界性的艺术之一

headline.core_cn: 世界性的；国际化的；见多识广的
headline.core_en: familiar with many cultures; containing or influenced by people and ideas from many countries

familiar_bridge.summary: cosmopolitan 不只是 global；它常说城市、人或艺术具有多文化、国际化气质。

core.main: cosmopolitan 强调跨文化、开放、国际化，而不是局限于本地。

usage_groups.group_title: 城市、人群或文化具有国际化气质
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 包含多国文化，或熟悉不同国家和文化。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 城市、人口、艺术、生活方式和人物评价中常见。

examples.sentence: New York is a cosmopolitan city shaped by immigrants from many countries.
examples.translation: 纽约是一座由来自许多国家的移民塑造的国际化城市。
examples.teaching_note: cosmopolitan city 指多文化城市。

examples.sentence: Her cosmopolitan outlook made her comfortable in almost any country.
examples.translation: 她国际化的视野使她几乎在任何国家都能适应。
examples.teaching_note: 可形容人的视野或品味。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The conference attracted an international audience.
He had a worldly confidence after years abroad.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: worldly 可指见多识广，也可能带世故感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The diplomat was urbane and tactful.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: urbane 更强调城市化的优雅和圆熟。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The company built a global brand.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: global 强调覆盖全球，不一定有文化开放感。

antonyms.example_sentence: The review criticized the museum’s provincial attitude.
antonyms.difference_note: provincial 与 cosmopolitan 的开放国际化相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: An insular culture resists outside influence.

word_family.example_sentence: The city aspired to become a modern cosmopolis.
word_family.difference_note: 较少见，但帮助理解词义。

word_family.example_sentence: Cosmos gives the word a sense of the wider world.

related_words.example_sentence: The metropolitan area contains many communities.
related_words.difference_note: 与城市和国际化语境常相邻。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 互相依赖的 (of two or more people or things) dependent on each other: interdependent economies 互相依赖的经济体 25044 interrelated

headline.core_en: depending on each other

familiar_bridge.summary: inter- 是“相互之间”；interdependent 就是彼此都离不开对方。

core.main: interdependent 强调两方或多方互相影响、互相依赖，不是单向依赖。

usage_groups.group_title: 系统、经济体或群体彼此依赖
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 每一方都影响另一方，不能完全独立运作。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 经济、生态、社会系统和国际关系中常见。

examples.sentence: Modern economies are increasingly interdependent.
examples.translation: 现代经济体越来越相互依赖。
examples.teaching_note: interdependent economies 是典型搭配。

examples.sentence: The organs of the body form an interdependent system.
examples.translation: 人体器官构成一个相互依赖的系统。
examples.teaching_note: 强调部分之间相互支撑。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The two problems are connected.
The two teams are dependent on each other for success.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The species are mutually dependent for survival.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调双方依赖。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The issues are complex and interrelated.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: interrelated 更强调关联，不一定依赖。

antonyms.example_sentence: The two studies reached independent conclusions.
The village tried to become self-sufficient.

word_family.example_sentence: Children depend on adults for care.
Economic dependence can limit political freedom.
Global interdependence makes isolation difficult.

related_words.example_sentence: The chemicals interact in complex ways.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: vi， 管闲事; 干涉他人事物 interfere in or busy oneself unduly with something that is not one's concern: She is always trying to meddle. 她老是想管闲事 。 | Stop meddling in my personal affairs. 别再干预我的私事

headline.core_en: to interfere in something that is not your concern

familiar_bridge.summary: meddle 不是 help，而是别人没请你管，你偏要插手。

core.main: meddle 常带负面语气，表示不恰当地干预他人的事情。

usage_groups.group_title: 不该插手却干涉别人的事
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调越界、管闲事。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 家庭、个人事务、政治和组织关系中常见。

examples.sentence: Stop meddling in my personal affairs.
examples.translation: 别再干涉我的私事。

examples.sentence: The mayor warned outside groups not to meddle in the election.
examples.translation: 市长警告外部团体不要干预选举。
examples.teaching_note: 政治语境中也可用 meddle in。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The parents tried not to interfere in the dispute.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: interfere 比 meddle 更中性一些。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He did not want to intrude on their conversation.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: intrude 强调侵入私人空间或谈话。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The court intervened to protect the child.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: intervene 可正面也可中性，meddle 多负面。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Someone tampered with the evidence.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: tamper 强调不当改动。

antonyms.example_sentence: She refrained from commenting on the dispute.
A good manager respects boundaries.
antonyms.difference_note: 与 meddle 的越界相反。

word_family.example_sentence: No one wanted a meddler in the family dispute.
The neighbors found him meddlesome.

related_words.example_sentence: meddle and middle look similar, but meanings differ.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 繁重的; 艰难的; 累人的 physically or mentally demanding: They look forward to retirement as a release from dull and taxing jobs. 他们盼望退休以摆脱枯燥而繁重的工作 。 近义词 arduous, strenuous, burdensome, exacting

headline.core_cn: 费力的；艰难的；令人疲惫的
headline.core_en: physically or mentally demanding

familiar_bridge.summary: taxing 像“消耗你的精力”：任务、工作或过程让人很累。

core.main: taxing 常形容工作、考试、过程、责任等对体力或脑力要求高。

usage_groups.group_title: 任务或过程很耗费体力或脑力
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调负担重、需要持续努力。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 工作、学习、政策执行和生活压力语境中常见。

examples.sentence: The nurses worked long hours in physically taxing conditions.
examples.translation: 护士们在体力消耗很大的条件下长时间工作。
examples.teaching_note: physically taxing 强调身体负担。

examples.sentence: Preparing the legal brief was a mentally taxing task.
examples.translation: 准备这份法律陈述是一项很耗脑力的任务。
examples.teaching_note: mentally taxing 强调脑力压力。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The exam was difficult for most students.
The hike was tiring but beautiful.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The team began the arduous climb before dawn.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: arduous 更正式，强调长时间艰苦。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The doctor advised him to avoid strenuous exercise.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: strenuous 常指体力强度大。

antonyms.example_sentence: The final step was easy.
Her performance looked effortless.

word_family.example_sentence: The crisis taxed the city’s resources.
word_family.difference_note: tax 作动词可表示消耗、使负担重。

word_family.example_sentence: Taxation became a central political issue.
word_family.difference_note: 税收义相关，但和 taxing 的“费力”义不同。

related_words.example_sentence: The editor had exacting standards.
related_words.difference_note: 近义语义场，强调要求高。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. jit (好东西 ) omit or decline to take (something pleasant or valuable); go without: forgo a holiday in order to finish a job 4 Y 5 LER FEMK A

headline.core_en: to choose not to have or do something pleasant or valuable

familiar_bridge.summary: forgo 常说为了更重要的目标，主动不享受某个好东西。

core.main: forgo 的重点是“本来可以要，但选择不要”，常带自愿牺牲。

usage_groups.group_title: 为了某个目标主动放弃好处或享受
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 放弃假期、机会、利润、舒适等有价值的东西。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 正式写作、商业、政策和个人选择语境中常见。

examples.sentence: She decided to forgo a holiday in order to finish the project.
examples.translation: 她决定放弃假期，以完成这个项目。
examples.teaching_note: forgo a holiday 是原书义项的自然表达。

examples.sentence: The company chose to forgo short-term profit to build public trust.
examples.translation: 公司选择放弃短期利润，以建立公众信任。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于权衡取舍。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He gave up dessert for a month.
She skipped lunch to catch the train.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: skip 更口语，未必有牺牲感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The prince renounced his claim to the throne.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: renounce 更正式，常指公开放弃权利或身份。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The leader refused to relinquish power.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: relinquish 常指放弃控制或权力。

antonyms.example_sentence: The company retained most of its profits.
Several groups claimed the reward.
antonyms.difference_note: claim 与不取、不享用相反。

word_family.example_sentence: A brief introduction may forego the main argument.
word_family.difference_note: forego 有时作 forgo 的变体，但传统上也有“先于”义。

related_words.example_sentence: Parents sometimes sacrifice comfort for their children.
related_words.difference_note: 和 forgo 的自愿放弃语义相连。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 极度愤慨; 极度愤怒 an extremely strong reaction of anger, shock, or indignation: Her voice trembled with outrage. #1 # & AWK i MF. v.t. 使极度愤慨;使极为愤怒 arouse fierce anger, shock, or indignation in (someone): His comments outraged nearly everyone in the room. 他的话激怒了几乎屋里的每个人

headline.core_en: intense anger or shock; to make people feel such anger

familiar_bridge.summary: outrage 是很强烈的愤怒，常带“这太不公/太过分”的道德反应。

core.main: outrage 可作名词表示公愤或愤慨，也可作动词表示使人极度愤怒。

usage_groups.group_title: 对不公或冒犯产生强烈愤慨
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调愤怒、震惊和道德谴责。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 新闻、政治、社会事件和公共舆论中常见。

examples.sentence: The decision sparked public outrage across the country.
examples.translation: 这一决定在全国引发公众愤慨。
examples.teaching_note: public outrage 表示公共层面的强烈愤怒。

examples.sentence: Her voice trembled with outrage as she described the injustice.
examples.translation: 她描述这件不公时，声音因愤慨而颤抖。
examples.teaching_note: with outrage 表示带着强烈愤怒。

usage_groups.group_title: 某言行激怒公众或群体
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 动词用法，表示让人感到震惊和愤怒。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 公共争议和评论语境中常见。

examples.sentence: His comments outraged nearly everyone in the room.
examples.translation: 他的话激怒了房间里几乎所有人。
examples.teaching_note: outrage someone 表示使某人极度愤怒。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The delay caused anger among passengers.
The report caused indignation among parents.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The verdict provoked fury in the crowd.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: fury 强调愤怒强度。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The leader faced the wrath of voters.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: wrath 更书面或文学。

antonyms.example_sentence: The plan won approval from local residents.
There was quiet acceptance of the compromise.
antonyms.difference_note: acceptance 强调接受而非愤怒。

word_family.example_sentence: Residents were outraged by the decision.
The price seemed outrageous.
word_family.difference_note: 形容词，表示过分到令人震惊。

related_words.example_sentence: She was indignant at the unfair accusation.

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 嘲笑 tease or laugh at in a scornful or contemptuous manner: We are being mocked for our religious beliefs. RA] A mae PZ AWK.

headline.core_en: to laugh at someone or something with contempt; not real but made to imitate something

familiar_bridge.summary: mock 作动词是“带轻蔑地嘲笑”；作形容词还可以表示“模拟的、假的”。

core.main: mock 的核心是“不认真对待”：要么嘲弄某人，要么做一个模拟版本。

usage_groups.group_title: 带轻蔑地嘲笑或讥讽
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调笑话、模仿或语言中带有轻视。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 人际冲突、社会评论和叙事中常见。

examples.sentence: The students were mocked for their unusual accents.
examples.translation: 这些学生因口音不同而受到嘲笑。
examples.teaching_note: be mocked for 表示因某事被嘲笑。

examples.sentence: The article mocked the mayor’s promise as unrealistic.
examples.translation: 这篇文章嘲讽市长的承诺不现实。
examples.teaching_note: mock an idea/promise 表示讥讽某个主张。

usage_groups.group_title: 模拟的、非真实的
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 表示为了练习或展示而仿造真实情境。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 考试、法庭、演练和教学中常见。

examples.sentence: The students took a mock exam before the real test.
examples.translation: 学生们在正式考试前参加了一次模拟考试。
examples.teaching_note: mock exam 是形容词用法。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The cartoon ridiculed the minister’s speech.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: ridicule 与 mock 的嘲笑义接近。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His classmates teased him about his haircut.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: tease 可轻可重，不一定恶意。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Critics derided the plan as naive.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: deride 更正式，轻蔑感强。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The child mimicked the teacher’s voice.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: mimic 是模仿，不一定嘲笑。

antonyms.example_sentence: The group respected his religious beliefs.
The review praised the novel’s originality.
antonyms.difference_note: praise 与 mock 的负面评价相反。

word_family.example_sentence: His mistake became the subject of mockery.
She spoke in a mocking tone.

related_words.example_sentence: Animal mimicry can protect a species.

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 嘲笑; i, teasing and contemptuous language or behavior directed at a particular person or thing: His kind of personality invites mockery. 他的个性招人嘲笑 。 近义词 derision

headline.core_en: ridicule or contemptuous treatment directed at someone or something

familiar_bridge.summary: mockery 是 mock 的名词：别人不是简单笑，而是带轻蔑地拿你当笑柄。

core.main: mockery 指嘲弄性的语言或行为，也可表示某事荒唐到像在嘲讽其本来目的。

usage_groups.group_title: 某人或某事成为嘲笑对象
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调轻蔑、讥讽和羞辱感。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 人物评价、公共舆论和叙事中常见。

examples.sentence: His awkward speech invited mockery from the crowd.
examples.translation: 他笨拙的讲话引来了人群的嘲笑。
examples.teaching_note: invite mockery 表示招来嘲弄。

examples.sentence: The committee greeted the proposal with mockery rather than serious debate.
examples.translation: 这项提议遭到嘲弄，而不是严肃讨论。
examples.teaching_note: be met with mockery 强调回应带轻蔑。

usage_groups.group_title: 某事荒唐到违背自身名义
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 表示“简直是对某原则的嘲弄”。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 评论法律、公平、民主等抽象原则时常见。

examples.sentence: The secret trial made a mockery of justice.
examples.translation: 秘密审判简直是对正义的嘲弄。
examples.teaching_note: make a mockery of 表示严重违背某原则。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The idea was exposed to ridicule.
The teasing stopped when the teacher entered.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The announcement was greeted with derision.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: derision 更正式，轻蔑感强。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She treated the excuse with scorn.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: scorn 强调蔑视。

antonyms.example_sentence: The speaker treated the opposing view with respect.
The scientist’s courage earned admiration.

word_family.example_sentence: The article mocked the policy.
A mocking laugh came from the back of the room.

related_words.example_sentence: The audience gave a derisive laugh.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. ， 狐烈批评; 公开辱骂 strong public criticism or verbal abuse: He endured years of contempt and obloquy. (th, & FRM, LE WAG ALF

headline.core_en: strong public criticism, abuse, or disgrace

familiar_bridge.summary: obloquy 比 criticism 更重，常是公开的羞辱、责骂和恶名。

core.main: obloquy 指一个人或群体承受的公开谴责和辱骂，语气正式。

usage_groups.group_title: 承受公开辱骂和强烈谴责
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调来自公众或舆论的强烈负面评价。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 历史、政治、传记和正式评论中常见。

examples.sentence: The reformer endured years of obloquy before his ideas were accepted.
examples.translation: 这位改革者在其思想被接受前忍受了多年的公开辱骂。
examples.teaching_note: endure obloquy 表示承受公开谴责。

examples.sentence: The scandal brought obloquy on the entire administration.
examples.translation: 这桩丑闻使整个政府蒙受恶名。
examples.teaching_note: bring obloquy on 表示招致恶名。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The proposal received sharp criticism.
The official faced verbal abuse from protesters.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The activist became the target of vilification.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: vilification 强调公开丑化。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The decision drew international opprobrium.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: opprobrium 与 obloquy 接近，正式。

antonyms.example_sentence: The decision won praise from voters.
The film received critical acclaim.

word_family.example_sentence: The loquacious guest dominated the dinner conversation.
word_family.difference_note: 同源于“说话”语义，但现代含义不同。

word_family.example_sentence: Her eloquent defense moved the audience.

related_words.example_sentence: The accusation amounted to slander.

source.raw_ocr_text: vt. 使放心; 使消除疑虑 say or do something to remove the doubts and fears of someone: They need to be reassured by parents and teachers that they are still wanted. 他们需要由家长和教师再三给以保证 ， 使之相信自己不是多余的人 。 | His remarks reassured me. 听了他的话

headline.core_en: to make someone feel less worried or doubtful

familiar_bridge.summary: assure 是“保证”；reassure 是在别人担心时，再给他安全感。

core.main: reassure 的重点是通过话语、行动或证据减轻别人的担心。

usage_groups.group_title: 用话语或行动让人安心
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 消除怀疑、焦虑或恐惧。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 家庭、教育、医疗、公共沟通和新闻中常见。

examples.sentence: The doctor reassured the patient that the pain was temporary.
examples.translation: 医生让病人放心，说疼痛只是暂时的。

examples.sentence: Officials tried to reassure the public after the accident.
examples.translation: 事故发生后，官员试图安抚公众。
examples.teaching_note: 公共沟通中常用 reassure the public。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She comforted the child after the nightmare.
The teacher calmed the students before the exam.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The new evidence allayed fears about the vaccine.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: allay 常接 fears/concerns。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He assured us that the plan was safe.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: assure 是保证；reassure 是在担心后使安心。

antonyms.example_sentence: The sudden noise alarmed the passengers.
The rumors unsettled the investors.

word_family.example_sentence: The manager assured the team that funding was secure.
Her words gave him reassurance.
The test results were reassuring.

related_words.example_sentence: The family wanted to feel sure that he was safe.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 巴结的; 53 HEM); 奉承拍马的 obedient or attentive to an excessive or servile degree: an obsequious compliment #438 74 | His obsequious submission to his boss's ideas disgusted his fellow workers. (i M27 BLE RED, RNA SRB.

headline.core_en: too eager to please or obey someone powerful

familiar_bridge.summary: obsequious 不是 polite，而是为了讨好权势者而过分顺从。

core.main: obsequious 常批评人奉承、卑躬屈膝、没有独立立场。

usage_groups.group_title: 对权势者过分顺从和奉承
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调讨好、巴结和奴性姿态。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 职场、政治、人物描写和讽刺评论中常见。

examples.sentence: His obsequious praise of the director embarrassed his colleagues.
examples.translation: 他对主管谄媚的赞美让同事们感到尴尬。
examples.teaching_note: obsequious praise 表示过分讨好。

examples.sentence: The minister’s obsequious manner made him appear weak rather than loyal.
examples.translation: 这位部长卑躬屈膝的姿态让他显得软弱，而不是忠诚。
examples.teaching_note: obsequious manner 强调姿态。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The assistant made flattering comments about every decision.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: flattering 可正可负，obsequious 更负面。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The dog became submissive after training.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: submissive 强调服从，不一定奉承。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The courtier’s servile behavior angered the reformers.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: servile 与 obsequious 很接近。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The leader surrounded himself with sycophantic advisers.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: sycophantic 强调谄媚讨好。

antonyms.example_sentence: She offered an independent judgment.
His forthright criticism surprised the committee.

word_family.example_sentence: His obsequiousness irritated the staff.
He bowed obsequiously before the official.

related_words.example_sentence: A ruler surrounded by sycophants hears little truth.

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.raw_ocr_text: 3444) 7X n. (#46 returns) (投资等的 ) 回报; 收益 a profit from an investment: produce maximum returns 创造最大的盈利

headline.core_en: profit from an investment; also the act of coming back

familiar_bridge.summary: return 在投资语境里不是“回来”，而是钱或投入带来的“回报”。

core.main: 本词在原书重点是投资收益；日常义“返回”只是熟词背景。

usage_groups.group_title: 投资或投入带来的收益
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 表示投入后得到的利润、收益或效果。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 商业、经济、政策评估和投资语境中常见。

examples.sentence: The fund produced strong returns for long-term investors.
examples.translation: 这只基金为长期投资者带来了可观收益。
examples.teaching_note: returns 常用复数表示投资收益。

examples.sentence: The program delivered social returns that were not measured in money.
examples.translation: 这个项目产生了不能用金钱衡量的社会回报。
examples.teaching_note: return 也可指非金钱回报。

usage_groups.group_title: 返回原处或恢复原状态
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 日常基础义，表示回到某地或某种状态。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 叙事和普通交流中常见。

examples.sentence: After months abroad, she returned home with new ideas.
examples.translation: 在国外几个月后，她带着新想法回到家。
examples.teaching_note: 基础义帮助理解“回到”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The business made a small profit in its first year.
The change brought real gains for workers.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The investment yielded steady income.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: yield 常用于投资或生产。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy paid political dividends.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: dividend 可引申为回报。

antonyms.example_sentence: The company reported a loss for the quarter.
The costs outweighed the returns.

word_family.example_sentence: The bottles are returnable for a small deposit.
Returnees brought new skills to the region.

related_words.example_sentence: Higher yields attracted investors.
related_words.difference_note: 投资语境里的重要近邻。

source.raw_ocr_text: Vit. [i] XIE; 为 … 喝彩; PH praise enthusiastically and publicly: acclaim the heroes 向英雄们欢呼 | a book widely acclaimed 一部受到广泛赞扬的书 2X44 hail

headline.core_en: to praise enthusiastically and publicly; enthusiastic public praise

familiar_bridge.summary: acclaim 是公开、热烈的赞扬，不只是普通 praise。

core.main: acclaim 常用于书、电影、人物或成就受到公众或评论界高度称赞。

usage_groups.group_title: 公开而热烈地称赞某人或作品
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调赞扬公开、强烈，常来自公众或评论界。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 艺术、文学、电影、公共人物评价中常见。

examples.sentence: The novel received international acclaim for its bold style.
examples.translation: 这部小说因大胆的风格获得国际赞誉。
examples.teaching_note: receive acclaim 常用于作品获赞。

examples.sentence: The director was widely acclaimed after her first film.
examples.translation: 这位导演在第一部电影后受到广泛赞誉。
examples.teaching_note: widely acclaimed 是常见被动表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Critics praised the performance.
The audience applauded the singer.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: applaud 可指实际鼓掌或称赞。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The discovery was hailed as a breakthrough.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: hail 常接 as，表示高度评价。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report lauded the team’s courage.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: laud 较正式。

antonyms.example_sentence: Reviewers criticized the weak ending.
The policy was condemned by rights groups.

word_family.example_sentence: The winner was greeted with acclamation.
word_family.difference_note: 名词形式，强调热烈赞同。

word_family.example_sentence: She is an acclaimed historian.

related_words.example_sentence: Acclaim is not just claim; it means public praise.
related_words.difference_note: 拼写联想，帮助避免混淆。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 喝彩; 热烈欢迎; 热情称赞 loud and enthusiastic approval, typically to welcome or honor someone or something: The president was again greeted by the acclamations of all present. 总统再次得到了全场人员的热烈欢迎

headline.core_en: loud and enthusiastic approval, welcome, or praise

familiar_bridge.summary: acclamation 是 acclaim 的名词，更强调一群人热烈欢迎或称赞的声音和场面。

core.main: acclamation 常描写公众或群体对某人、决定或胜利的热烈赞同。

usage_groups.group_title: 人群热烈欢迎、称赞或赞同
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调公开、集体、热烈的认可。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 政治、仪式、历史叙述和人物评价中常见。

examples.sentence: The candidate was chosen by acclamation after no one opposed the nomination.
examples.translation: 在无人反对提名后，这名候选人以鼓掌通过的方式当选。
examples.teaching_note: by acclamation 常指无需投票、以欢呼赞同通过。

examples.sentence: The returning team was greeted with acclamation in the town square.
examples.translation: 归来的队伍在镇广场受到热烈欢迎。
examples.teaching_note: greeted with acclamation 强调热烈欢迎。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The speech ended in long applause.
The rescue workers received praise.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The pianist received a standing ovation.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: ovation 强调热烈鼓掌欢呼。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The book won immediate acclaim.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: acclaim 更常指赞誉本身。

antonyms.example_sentence: The decision met with public disapproval.
The attack drew widespread condemnation.

word_family.example_sentence: Critics acclaimed the film.
The acclaimed poet visited the university.

related_words.example_sentence: The crowd’s clamor filled the hall.
related_words.difference_note: 声音意象相关，但含义不同。

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. FEAR; 把 … 理解成 … interpret (a word or action) in a particular way: construe one's letter literally 按字面意义解释某人的信件 | His withdrawal was not to be construed as a protest. 他的退场不应该被看作是一种抗议 。 3K. misconstrue /misken'struy/ v. 误解 interpret (something, especially a person's words or actions) wrongly: /'m sorry you misconstrued my meaning. 你误解我的意思了 ， 我感到遗憾

headline.core_en: to interpret a word, action, or situation in a particular way

familiar_bridge.summary: construe 不是简单 understand，而是“把某句话/行为解释成某种意思”。

core.main: construe 常用于法律、文本、言论和行为解释，强调解释角度。

usage_groups.group_title: 把话语、行为或文本解释成某种意思
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调解释方式，而不是事情本身。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 法律、阅读理解、正式写作和评论中常见。

examples.sentence: His silence should not be construed as agreement.
examples.translation: 他的沉默不应被理解为同意。

examples.sentence: The court construed the clause narrowly.
examples.translation: 法院对该条款作了狭义解释。
examples.teaching_note: 法律语境中常用 construe。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Readers may interpret the ending differently.
I understood the remark as a joke.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The comment can be read as a warning.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: read as 在解释文本或行为时接近。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The lawyer parsed each sentence carefully.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: parse 更偏逐字分析结构。

antonyms.example_sentence: She worried that her silence would be misconstrued.
The data were misinterpreted by the committee.

word_family.example_sentence: Do not misconstrue caution as hostility.
The phrase admits several constructions.
word_family.difference_note: 正式语境中 construction 可指解释方式。

related_words.example_sentence: The way a sentence is structured affects how it is construed.
related_words.difference_note: 帮助理解 -stru- 的“建构/结构”感。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. YN; 难解的 difficult to understand; obscure: Philosophical semantics is too abstruse to understand. 哲学语义学派玄而又玄 ， 无法读懂

headline.core_en: difficult to understand because it is complex, obscure, or highly abstract

familiar_bridge.summary: abstruse 比 difficult 更书面，常说理论、论证、文字太抽象、太难懂。

core.main: abstruse 用来形容概念、理论、讨论或文字复杂到不容易理解。

usage_groups.group_title: 理论、文字或讨论深奥难懂
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调抽象、复杂、晦涩。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 哲学、理论、学术写作和评论中常见。

examples.sentence: The article was filled with abstruse terminology that discouraged general readers.
examples.translation: 这篇文章充满深奥术语，让普通读者望而却步。
examples.teaching_note: abstruse terminology 强调术语难懂。

examples.sentence: The professor turned an abstruse philosophical problem into a clear classroom discussion.
examples.translation: 教授把一个深奥的哲学问题讲成了清楚的课堂讨论。
examples.teaching_note: 常修饰理论或问题。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The chapter was difficult for beginners.
The instructions were hard to understand.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The poem’s meaning remains obscure.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: obscure 强调不清楚。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The seminar focused on esoteric legal questions.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: esoteric 强调只有少数人懂。

antonyms.example_sentence: The explanation was clear and helpful.
The book makes physics accessible to beginners.
antonyms.difference_note: accessible 强调容易接近或理解。

word_family.example_sentence: The theory deals in high levels of abstraction.
word_family.difference_note: 与 abstruse 的抽象难懂感相关。

word_family.example_sentence: The essay is too abstract for young readers.

related_words.example_sentence: The jargon obscured the argument.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 反对 ( 权威意见 ) 的; 持不同政见的 in opposition to official policy: dissident scholars 持不同政见的学者

headline.core_cn: 持不同政见的；反对官方立场的
headline.core_en: opposed to official policy, authority, or accepted doctrine

familiar_bridge.summary: dissident 里的 dis- 有“不一致、分开”的感觉；它常指与官方或主流立场不一致的人。

core.main: dissident 强调反对权威、官方政策或正统观点，常见于政治和思想语境。

usage_groups.group_title: 反对官方政策或权威观点
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 表示公开或持续地持不同立场。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 政治、历史、学术和思想争论中常见。

examples.sentence: Dissident scholars challenged the official version of history.
examples.translation: 持不同意见的学者挑战了官方历史叙述。
examples.teaching_note: dissident scholars 是原书义项的自然表达。

examples.sentence: The government arrested several political dissidents after the protest.
examples.translation: 抗议之后，政府逮捕了几名持不同政见者。
examples.teaching_note: 作名词时指持不同政见者。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Opposing voices grew louder after the scandal.

basic_synonyms.difference_note: critical 不一定涉及政治反对。

advanced_synonyms.meaning: 不随俗的；不遵从传统的
advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The artist was a nonconformist in both style and politics.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: nonconformist 强调不顺从规范。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His heretical views angered the church authorities.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: heretical 常用于宗教或思想正统语境。

antonyms.example_sentence: Loyalists defended the government’s decision.

antonyms.example_sentence: A conformist rarely challenges accepted rules.

word_family.example_sentence: Several judges wrote a dissent.

related_words.example_sentence: Experts disagree about the cause.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 难以捉摸的; 不受控制的; 任性的 difficult to control or predict because of unusual or perverse behavior: the wayward flight of some birds 某些鸟类难以捉摸的飞行

headline.core_cn: 任性的；不受控制的；难以预测的
headline.core_en: difficult to control or predict because of stubborn or irregular behavior

familiar_bridge.summary: wayward 像“不按路走”：人、行为或运动轨迹偏离正常方向。

core.main: wayward 常形容孩子、性格、行为、飞行轨迹等不听管束或难以预测。

usage_groups.group_title: 人或行为任性、不受控制
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调偏离规则、纪律或预期。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 人物描写、教育、家庭和社会评论中常见。

examples.sentence: The teacher tried to guide a wayward student without humiliating him.
examples.translation: 老师试图引导一个任性的学生，同时不羞辱他。
examples.teaching_note: wayward student/child 表示难管教。

examples.sentence: His wayward impulses often led him into trouble.
examples.translation: 他任性的冲动常让他惹上麻烦。
examples.teaching_note: 可形容冲动或行为。

usage_groups.group_title: 运动或方向难以预测
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 表示路线、飞行或发展不稳定。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 自然描写、运动轨迹和比喻表达中可见。

examples.sentence: The wayward flight of the bird made it hard to photograph.
examples.translation: 这只鸟忽左忽右的飞行让人很难拍到。
examples.teaching_note: 原书例子中的 flight 指不规则轨迹。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The unruly crowd ignored the guards.
The weather remained unpredictable all week.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The machine produced erratic results.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: erratic 更强调模式不规则。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The king was feared for his capricious judgments.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: capricious 更正式，偏任性变化。

antonyms.example_sentence: The dog was obedient after training.
The machine followed a predictable pattern.

word_family.example_sentence: Wayward literally suggests moving away from the expected way.
word_family.difference_note: 帮助理解“偏离路径”的画面。

word_family.example_sentence: His waywardness worried his parents.

related_words.example_sentence: To err is to go wrong.
related_words.difference_note: 与“偏离正路”的意象相近。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. (对于酒 、 色等的 ) 节制; 禁欲 the fact or practice of restraining oneself from indulging in something, typically alcohol: complete abstinence from alcohol 滴酒不沾

headline.core_en: the practice of deliberately not doing or taking something, especially alcohol, sex, or an indulgence

familiar_bridge.summary: abstinence 不是普通 self-control，而是有意识地完全不碰某种享受或欲望。

core.main: abstinence 常用于戒酒、禁欲、饮食控制或道德/宗教节制。

usage_groups.group_title: 主动戒除酒精、性或其他享受
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调自我克制，通常是完全不做。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 健康、宗教、伦理和个人习惯语境中常见。

examples.sentence: The doctor recommended complete abstinence from alcohol during treatment.
examples.translation: 医生建议治疗期间完全戒酒。

examples.sentence: The community valued fasting and abstinence as forms of discipline.
examples.translation: 这个社群把斋戒和节制视为自律形式。
examples.teaching_note: 可用于宗教或道德语境。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Self-control helped him avoid old habits.
The leader showed restraint during the crisis.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: restraint 指控制冲动或行为。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Temperance movements opposed the sale of alcohol.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: temperance 常与戒酒、节制有关。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The monk’s renunciation of luxury impressed visitors.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: renunciation 更正式，强调主动弃绝。

antonyms.example_sentence: The trip was a rare indulgence.
Excess can damage both health and judgment.

word_family.example_sentence: She abstained from alcohol for a year.
An abstinent lifestyle can be difficult to maintain.

related_words.example_sentence: Both words carry an idea of holding back or containing an impulse.

headline.core_en: a natural tendency or inclination to do or like something

familiar_bridge.summary: proclivity 比 tendency 更书面，常说一个人反复表现出的偏好或倾向。

core.main: proclivity 通常指较稳定的个人倾向，有时带轻微负面色彩。

usage_groups.group_title: 某人稳定地倾向于做某事
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调反复出现的偏好、习性或倾向。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 人物分析、心理、评论和正式写作中常见。

examples.sentence: He has a proclivity for finding fault with every proposal.
examples.translation: 他有一种对每个提议都挑毛病的倾向。
examples.teaching_note: a proclivity for doing 表示习惯性倾向。

examples.sentence: The essay examines humanity’s proclivity to explain chance as destiny.
examples.translation: 这篇文章考察人类把偶然解释为命运的倾向。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She has a tendency to speak too quickly.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The critic had a predilection for experimental fiction.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The material has a propensity to crack under pressure.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: propensity 可用于人或物的倾向。

antonyms.example_sentence: She had an aversion to public speaking.
antonyms.difference_note: aversion 是避开某事的倾向。

antonyms.example_sentence: His disinclination to compromise delayed the talks.

word_family.example_sentence: The evidence inclined him to agree.

word_family.example_sentence: The child showed a propensity for music.

core.main: mercurial 强调变化快、难预测，多用于性情、情绪、风格或环境。

source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 阻碍; 妨碍; 阻止 to make (something, such as a task or action) slow or difficult: Bad weather hindered travel. 天气不好阻碍了旅行 。 | You're hindering me in my work by talking all the time. 你讲个不停 ， 妨碍了我工作 。 近义词 block, inhibit, stymie

headline.core_en: to make progress, action, or work slower or more difficult

familiar_bridge.summary: hinder 不是完全 block，而是让事情变慢、变难、推进受阻。

core.main: hinder 常接 progress, travel, work, development 等，强调造成障碍。

usage_groups.group_title: 让行动、进展或工作变慢变难
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 阻碍但不一定完全阻止。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 工作、交通、研究、政策和发展语境中常见。

examples.sentence: Bad weather hindered travel across the region.
examples.translation: 恶劣天气阻碍了整个地区的出行。
examples.teaching_note: hinder travel 是原书义项的自然表达。

examples.sentence: Lack of reliable data hindered the researchers’ analysis.
examples.translation: 缺乏可靠数据妨碍了研究者的分析。
examples.teaching_note: hinder analysis/progress 表示让工作推进困难。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The storm slowed the rescue effort.
Fallen trees blocked the road.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Bureaucracy impeded reform.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: impede 更正式，接近 hinder。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Legal disputes stymied the project.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: stymie 强调让计划卡住。

antonyms.example_sentence: The new system facilitated communication.
The agency expedited the approval process.

word_family.example_sentence: The rule became a hindrance to innovation.
The team continued unhindered.
word_family.difference_note: un- 表示没有受到阻碍。

related_words.example_sentence: The old root relates to being behind or held back.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 高度赞扬 praise highly in speech or writing: eulogizes the workers ## TA i£ X44] lionize

headline.core_en: to praise someone or something highly, especially in speech or writing

familiar_bridge.summary: eulogy 是悼词或颂词；eulogize 就是用正式话语高度赞扬。

core.main: eulogize 常用于演讲、文章、悼念或公共评价，比普通 praise 更正式。

usage_groups.group_title: 在演讲或文字中高度赞扬
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调公开、正式、郑重地称赞。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 悼词、传记、公共演讲、历史评论中常见。

examples.sentence: The mayor eulogized the firefighters for their courage.
examples.translation: 市长赞扬消防员的勇气。
examples.teaching_note: eulogize someone for something 表示正式称赞。

examples.sentence: At the memorial, friends eulogized her generosity and humor.
examples.translation: 在追悼会上，朋友们称颂她的慷慨和幽默。
examples.teaching_note: 在悼念场景中非常常见。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The teacher praised the student’s effort.
The city honored the volunteers.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Critics lauded the novel’s style.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: laud 更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The essay extols the virtues of patience.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: extol 强调高度赞扬。

antonyms.example_sentence: The review criticized the weak argument.
The senator denounced the policy as unjust.

word_family.example_sentence: He delivered a moving eulogy for his teacher.
The biography is respectful but not merely eulogistic.

related_words.example_sentence: Both begin with eu-, meaning good or favorable.
related_words.difference_note: eu- 可提示“好的、正面的”。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 话多的; WENA (RAY excessively talkative, especially on trivial matters: garrulous speeches OF OR BA I i 3044] loquacious 派生 garrulity /gz'ru:loti/ n. 话多; WEMEA KR excessive talkativeness, especially on trivial matters: the character's comic garrulity 角色带有喜剧色彩的喉喉不休

headline.core_en: excessively talkative, especially about trivial matters

familiar_bridge.summary: garrulous 不是 friendly talkative，而是话太多、常说些琐碎内容。

core.main: garrulous 常批评一个人或文风冗长、啰嗦、停不下来。

usage_groups.group_title: 人说话太多而且多琐碎
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调过度健谈，甚至令人疲惫。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 人物描写、文学评论和风格评价中常见。

examples.sentence: The garrulous passenger told stories for the entire flight.
examples.translation: 这位喋喋不休的乘客整个航程都在讲故事。
examples.teaching_note: garrulous person 强调话太多。

examples.sentence: The novel’s garrulous narrator interrupts the plot with endless anecdotes.
examples.translation: 这部小说喋喋不休的叙述者用没完没了的轶事打断情节。
examples.teaching_note: 可形容叙述风格啰嗦。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The child is talkative around friends.
The letter was warm and chatty.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The professor was brilliant but loquacious.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: loquacious 较正式，接近 garrulous。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report was verbose and hard to follow.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: verbose 多形容文字冗长。

antonyms.example_sentence: The taciturn farmer answered in short phrases.
She was reserved in public meetings.

word_family.example_sentence: The character’s comic garrulity made him memorable.
He spoke garrulously about his travels.

related_words.example_sentence: Her loquacious style filled every silence.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 窘境;无所适从 a state of perplexity or doubt: be in a quandary about what to do 不知怎么办才好 | putsb. in a quandary 使某人陷入蹇境 244 dilemma

headline.core_cn: 困境；左右为难；不知所措
headline.core_en: a state of uncertainty or difficulty about what to do

familiar_bridge.summary: quandary 是“不知道该怎么办”的困境，通常因为几个选择都不好。

core.main: quandary 强调选择困难和犹豫，不只是一般 problem。

usage_groups.group_title: 面对选择时左右为难
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不知道该采取哪种行动。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 决策、伦理困境、个人选择和评论中常见。

examples.sentence: The unexpected offer put her in a quandary about whether to stay or leave.
examples.translation: 这个意外的邀请让她陷入去留两难。

examples.sentence: The judge faced a moral quandary when the law and fairness seemed to conflict.
examples.translation: 当法律和公平似乎冲突时，法官面对道德困境。
examples.teaching_note: moral quandary 表示伦理上的两难。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The team faced a serious problem.
The plan ran into difficulty.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The doctor faced a dilemma about patient confidentiality.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: dilemma 强调两种选择都困难。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The lost hikers were in a dangerous predicament.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: predicament 强调处境麻烦。

antonyms.example_sentence: Certainty returned after the facts became clear.
A simple solution ended the debate.

word_family.example_sentence: A query can begin the process of resolving a quandary.

related_words.example_sentence: Doubt kept him in a quandary.

source.raw_ocr_text: #473) FL adj. 灵活的; 可塑的; 易变的 easily shaped or molded: plastic personality 易变的性格派生 plasticity /ple'stisiti/ n. 可塑性 the quality of being easily shaped or molded: the plasticity of clay 粘土的可塑性

headline.core_cn: 可塑的；易受影响的；塑料的
headline.core_en: easily shaped or molded; able to change; also made of plastic material

familiar_bridge.summary: plastic 最基本是“能被塑形”；从材料扩展到性格、头脑、制度的可塑性。

core.main: GRE 更常考抽象义：某物能被塑造、改变或适应，而不只是塑料。

usage_groups.group_title: 材料或事物容易被塑形
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调能被塑造、改变形状。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 材料、艺术、科学和比喻表达中常见。

examples.sentence: Clay remains plastic until it is fired in a kiln.
examples.translation: 黏土在窑中烧制之前保持可塑性。
examples.teaching_note: plastic clay 是具体材料义。

usage_groups.group_title: 性格、头脑或制度有可塑性
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调能被环境、训练或经验改变。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 心理学、教育、社会科学和评论中常见。

examples.sentence: Children’s brains are remarkably plastic in the early years.
examples.translation: 儿童早期的大脑具有惊人的可塑性。
examples.teaching_note: plastic brain 是抽象科学语境。

examples.sentence: A plastic personality may adapt quickly but also lack firm principles.
examples.translation: 可塑性强的性格可能适应很快，但也可能缺乏坚定原则。
examples.teaching_note: 原书义项可用于 personality。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The material is moldable when heated.
The schedule is flexible.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Young minds can be malleable.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: malleable 可用于金属或人。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The species is highly adaptable.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: adaptable 强调能适应环境。

antonyms.example_sentence: Rigid rules left no room for judgment.
His inflexible attitude slowed the negotiations.

word_family.example_sentence: The plasticity of the brain declines with age.
Additives can plasticize the material.

related_words.example_sentence: Education can mold a child’s habits.

source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 前弱; 破坏 weaken; undermine: undercut a plan 破坏计划 | undercut one's confidence AK 本上动摇某人的信心

headline.core_en: to weaken, damage, or sell at a lower price than a competitor

familiar_bridge.summary: under- 有“从下面”的感觉；undercut 就像从底下削掉支撑，让计划、信心或价格被削弱。

core.main: undercut 常表示削弱论点、破坏计划，也可表示用低价抢竞争者。

usage_groups.group_title: 削弱计划、论点、信心或权威
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 从根基上削弱，使某事不再稳固。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 论证、政策、心理和评论语境中常见。

examples.sentence: The new evidence undercut the witness’s credibility.
examples.translation: 新证据削弱了这名证人的可信度。
examples.teaching_note: undercut credibility 表示从根基上削弱可信度。

examples.sentence: Constant criticism undercut her confidence.
examples.translation: 持续的批评削弱了她的信心。
examples.teaching_note: undercut confidence 是原书义项的自然表达。

usage_groups.group_title: 用更低价格削弱竞争者
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 以较低价格抢走市场优势。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 商业和市场语境中常见。

examples.sentence: Online retailers undercut local shops by offering lower prices.
examples.translation: 网店以更低价格削弱了本地商店的竞争力。
examples.teaching_note: 商业义里 undercut 与价格竞争有关。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The scandal weakened public trust.
The rumor damaged his reputation.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The leaks undermined the peace talks.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: undermine 与 undercut 很接近。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Years of neglect eroded confidence in the system.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: erode 强调逐渐削弱。

antonyms.example_sentence: The evidence strengthened her argument.
The data supported the conclusion.

word_family.example_sentence: Budget cuts weakened the program.
Price undercutting hurt smaller firms.

related_words.example_sentence: The policy undermined local autonomy.
related_words.difference_note: 同属“从下面削支撑”的语义。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 有名的; 享有盛誉的 known or talked about by many people; famous: a renowned college 名牌学院 | be renowned as a portrait painter 以肖像画家的身份闻名 #044 celebrated, well-known, acclaimed 3x renown /rrnaun/ n. 名气; 声誉 the condition of being known or talked about by many people; fame: authors of great renown 负有盛名的作者

headline.core_en: widely known and admired

familiar_bridge.summary: renowned 不只是 famous；它通常带有“因成就而有声望”。

core.main: renowned 常形容学校、作家、艺术家、科学家或机构因品质和成就而闻名。

usage_groups.group_title: 因成就或品质而广为人知
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调名声和声望，多为正面。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 人物、机构、作品和地点评价中常见。

examples.sentence: The university is renowned for its medical research.
examples.translation: 这所大学以医学研究闻名。
examples.teaching_note: be renowned for 表示因某事闻名。

examples.sentence: She is renowned as a portrait painter.
examples.translation: 她以肖像画家身份享有盛名。
examples.teaching_note: be renowned as 表示以某身份闻名。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The singer became famous at a young age.
He is well-known in the local community.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: well-known 不一定带高度声望。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The celebrated architect designed the museum.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: celebrated 带赞赏。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: An eminent scientist chaired the panel.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: eminent 强调地位高、成就突出。

antonyms.example_sentence: The poet remained obscure during his lifetime.
The artist was unknown outside his village.

word_family.example_sentence: Authors of great renown gathered at the festival.
The form is rare; use renowned instead.

related_words.example_sentence: Her reputation grew after the discovery.

source.raw_ocr_text: vt. 扼要重述; 总结; 概括 summarize and state again the main points of: Can J just recapitulate the details of the plan? 我只把计划的细节扼要重复一遍可以吗 ?

headline.core_en: to summarize and state the main points again

familiar_bridge.summary: re- 是“再一次”；recapitulate 就是把重点再概括一遍。

core.main: recapitulate 常用于演讲、文章、课堂或报告结尾，重新梳理要点。

usage_groups.group_title: 重新概括主要内容
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 把已经说过的内容压缩成要点再说一遍。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 课堂、报告、会议和学术写作中常见。

examples.sentence: Let me briefly recapitulate the main points before we end.
examples.translation: 结束前我简要重述一下要点。

examples.sentence: The final chapter recapitulates the argument of the entire book.
examples.translation: 最后一章概括重述了整本书的论证。
examples.teaching_note: 学术写作中可用于章节总结。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The teacher summarized the lesson in five minutes.
Please repeat the question.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: repeat 只是再说，不一定概括。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The lawyer restated the central claim.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: restate 强调换句话再说。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The article reviews the evidence.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: review 可指回顾或评述。

antonyms.example_sentence: The speaker expanded the brief note into a full argument.
She elaborated on the plan.

word_family.example_sentence: The lecture ended with a short recapitulation.
The two words look related but meanings differ in modern use.

related_words.example_sentence: A good summary captures the main points.

source.raw_ocr_text: #74 ##XL adj. 刻意的; 精心营造的; 煞费苦心的 (of a quality or result) achieved or maintained by careful and deliberate effort: The government treated the news of defeat with studied indifference. 政府对战败的消息故意装得满不在乎 。 | What he said to me was a studied insult, 1 At RL AN HEE 7) eB He.

headline.core_en: deliberately done or carefully maintained, not spontaneous

familiar_bridge.summary: studied 在这里不是“被研究过的”，而是“刻意摆出来的”。

core.main: studied 常形容态度、冷漠、礼貌或侮辱是有意营造的，不是自然流露。

usage_groups.group_title: 态度或行为刻意营造出来
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调经过控制、设计或故意表现。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 人物描写、政治姿态和文学评论中常见。

examples.sentence: The minister greeted the criticism with studied indifference.
examples.translation: 部长对批评表现出刻意的冷漠。
examples.teaching_note: studied indifference 是原书义项的自然表达。

examples.sentence: His apology sounded like a studied performance rather than genuine regret.
examples.translation: 他的道歉听起来像刻意表演，而不是真诚后悔。
examples.teaching_note: studied 强调不自然、经过设计。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The pause was deliberate.
The omission was intentional.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her calculated silence made the room uneasy.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: calculated 更强调有策略。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The ceremony felt contrived, as if every gesture had been rehearsed.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: contrived 更强调做作、不自然；studied 更强调有意控制出来的姿态。

antonyms.example_sentence: The applause was spontaneous.
Her concern seemed genuine.

word_family.example_sentence: The studious child spent every evening reading.
word_family.difference_note: 同词族，但 studied 在本词中表示“刻意营造的”，不是“用功的”。

word_family.example_sentence: She studiously avoided the topic during the interview.
word_family.difference_note: 副词形式，常表示有意回避或认真维持某种姿态。

related_words.example_sentence: The decision followed long deliberation.

source.raw_ocr_text: ad. (言辞 、 文字 ) THRIMEALAY; 不切题的 (of writing or speech) lengthy and confused or inconsequential: J am sorry for this rambling letter. 写了这么一封杂乱无章的信 ， 对不起 。 近头词 long-winded

headline.core_cn: 漫无边际的；杂乱冗长的
headline.core_en: lengthy, confused, and not well organized, especially of speech or writing

familiar_bridge.summary: rambling 像走路乱绕：说话或写作绕来绕去，没有清楚主线。

core.main: rambling 常形容信、演讲、文章或回答冗长散乱、不切题。

usage_groups.group_title: 言辞或文字冗长散乱、不切题
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调缺少结构，绕来绕去。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 写作评价、演讲评价和叙事中常见。

examples.sentence: I am sorry for this rambling letter; I wrote it in a hurry.
examples.translation: 抱歉写了这封杂乱冗长的信；我写得太匆忙了。
examples.teaching_note: rambling letter 是原书义项的自然表达。

examples.sentence: The witness gave a rambling answer that confused the jury.
examples.translation: 证人给出了一个东拉西扯的回答，让陪审团困惑。
examples.teaching_note: rambling answer 强调回答没有条理。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The email was long but clear.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: long 只说长度，不一定混乱。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His explanation was confused.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The lecture was long-winded and repetitive.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: long-winded 强调说得太长。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The essay was meandering but occasionally insightful.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: meandering 像路线曲折，常指文章或谈话散。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her answer was concise and accurate.
The argument was coherent and persuasive.

word_family.example_sentence: He rambled for twenty minutes before reaching the point.
The rambler moved slowly along the path.

related_words.example_sentence: The talk wandered from the main topic.
related_words.difference_note: 帮助理解“偏离主线”的画面。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 字面的; 单词字面意思的 taking words in their usual or most basic sense without metaphor or allegory: The literal meaning of “know your ropes” is to know a lot about ropes, while figuratively it means to know a lot about how to do something. 词组 know your ropes 字面意 Be “meet”. Whe “Re, BAR”.

headline.core_en: using words in their basic meaning, not figurative or metaphorical

familiar_bridge.summary: literal 是“按字面理解”；它和 figurative（比喻的）正好形成对照。

core.main: literal 常用于解释词语、翻译、意义或理解方式，强调不作比喻引申。

usage_groups.group_title: 按词语最基本的字面意思理解
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不按比喻、象征或夸张理解。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 语言学习、阅读、翻译和解释语境中常见。

examples.sentence: The literal meaning of the phrase is different from its idiomatic meaning.
examples.translation: 这个短语的字面意思和惯用意思不同。

examples.sentence: He took the joke literally and became confused.
examples.translation: 他把这个笑话按字面理解，因此困惑了。
examples.teaching_note: take literally 表示按字面理解。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The exact wording matters in law.
The translation was almost word-for-word.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: word-for-word 与 literal translation 接近。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The witness repeated the statement verbatim.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: verbatim 更正式，表示逐字照搬。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The term has a nonfigurative meaning here.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 直接对应不使用比喻。

antonyms.example_sentence: The phrase has a figurative meaning.
The “journey” is metaphorical, not literal.

word_family.example_sentence: She meant the statement literally.
The poem plays with literality and metaphor.

related_words.example_sentence: literal originally relates to letters.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 正直; 诚实 the quality of being honest and having strong moral principles; moral uprightness: a man of integrity 刚正不阿的人

headline.core_en: honesty and strong moral principles; also wholeness or structural soundness

familiar_bridge.summary: integrity 一方面是人的道德完整，一方面是结构或系统没有破损。

core.main: integrity 在 GRE 中既可指品格正直，也可指体系、数据、建筑等保持完整可靠。

usage_groups.group_title: 人的正直和道德原则
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调诚实、不背叛原则。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 人物评价、伦理、公共生活中常见。

examples.sentence: The judge was respected as a person of integrity.
examples.translation: 这位法官因正直而受人尊敬。
examples.teaching_note: a person of integrity 是原书义项的自然表达。

examples.sentence: Professional integrity required her to report the error.
examples.translation: 职业操守要求她报告这个错误。
examples.teaching_note: professional integrity 指职业伦理。

usage_groups.group_title: 结构、系统或数据保持完整可靠
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调没有被破坏、篡改或分裂。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 科技、工程、学术和制度语境中常见。

examples.sentence: Engineers tested the bridge’s structural integrity after the earthquake.
examples.translation: 地震后，工程师检测了桥梁的结构完整性。
examples.teaching_note: structural integrity 是工程语境。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her honesty earned the team’s trust.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: honesty 对应品格上的诚实；integrity 还强调原则完整。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The restoration preserved the wholeness of the design.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The official was known for probity.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: probity 更正式，强调廉洁正直。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report questioned the soundness of the method.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: soundness 对应结构或方法可靠。

antonyms.example_sentence: Corruption destroyed public trust.
Fragmentation weakened the system.

word_family.example_sentence: Trust is integral to cooperation.
word_family.difference_note: 同根，表示构成整体不可缺。

word_family.example_sentence: The school integrated new students into the program.
word_family.difference_note: 动词形式，表示合成整体。

word_family.example_sentence: Integer shares the idea of wholeness.

related_words.example_sentence: The entire structure remained intact.

source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 减少; 削减 reduce or diminish (something) in extent or quantity: Right-wing parties which seek to retrench the welfare state. & BK, 4 BIR BAI. 近义词 curtail

headline.core_en: to reduce spending, services, or activities in order to save money or resources

familiar_bridge.summary: retrench 常出现在经济、政府、公司语境：资源不够时收缩、削减。

core.main: retrench 的核心是缩减规模或开支，通常是为了应对压力。

usage_groups.group_title: 削减开支、服务或规模
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 通过收缩来节省资源。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 经济、政治、企业管理和政策语境中常见。

examples.sentence: The government tried to retrench welfare spending during the crisis.
examples.translation: 危机期间，政府试图削减福利开支。
examples.teaching_note: retrench spending/services 是核心用法。

examples.sentence: Facing lower demand, the company retrenched and closed several offices.
examples.translation: 面对需求下降，公司收缩业务并关闭了几个办事处。
examples.teaching_note: 公司语境中可表示收缩。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The city reduced bus service at night.
The family cut back on spending.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy curtailed public access to the records.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: curtail 更正式，常指限制。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The firm downsized after losing major clients.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: downsize 常用于公司裁员或缩小规模。

antonyms.example_sentence: The agency expanded its services.
The budget increased this year.

word_family.example_sentence: Retrenchment became unavoidable after revenue fell.
The root image suggests cutting back or cutting away.

related_words.example_sentence: Austerity measures reduced public spending.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 好斗的 eager or quick to argue or fight; aggressively defiant: a truculent person 好斗的人 3& 3G] pugnacious

headline.core_en: eager to argue or fight; aggressively defiant

familiar_bridge.summary: truculent 比 aggressive 更带“随时要吵/要打”的挑衅感。

core.main: truculent 常形容人、态度、语气或回应好斗、咄咄逼人。

word_family.example_sentence: His truculence alienated potential allies.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 权宜之计; 临时的解决办法 an improvised substitute for something lacking; a temporary expedient: The export of goods and lending of money alone are only emergency stopgaps. 出口货物和借出资金只不过是权宜之计

headline.core_cn: 权宜之计；临时替代办法
headline.core_en: a temporary solution used until something better is available

familiar_bridge.summary: stopgap 就像先“堵住缺口”：不是最终方案，只是临时顶上。

core.main: stopgap 常形容临时政策、资金、人员或办法，用来应急。

usage_groups.group_title: 临时解决眼前问题
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 暂时填补空缺，不是长期方案。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 政策、商业、管理和日常问题解决中常见。

examples.sentence: The loan was only a stopgap until the company found new investors.
examples.translation: 这笔贷款只是公司找到新投资者前的临时办法。
examples.teaching_note: only a stopgap 强调非长期方案。

examples.sentence: Emergency imports served as a stopgap during the shortage.
examples.translation: 短缺期间，紧急进口充当了权宜之计。
examples.teaching_note: 可用于经济和政策语境。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The tent was a temporary solution after the flood.
The teacher found a substitute for the missing material.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The compromise was a political expedient.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: expedient 与 stopgap 接近，但更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: They built a makeshift shelter from branches.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: makeshift 强调临时拼凑。

antonyms.example_sentence: The city needed a permanent solution to flooding.
A long-term plan replaced the emergency measure.

word_family.example_sentence: A stopgap stops a gap temporarily.

related_words.example_sentence: The makeshift bridge lasted only a week.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 告知; 使了解; 给予知识 give (someone) greater knowledge and understanding about a subject or situation: Would you enlighten me on your plans for the future? 能否请你给我讲讲你未来的计划 ?

headline.core_en: to give someone knowledge, understanding, or clearer insight

familiar_bridge.summary: light 是“光”；enlighten 像把光照进来，让人更明白。

core.main: enlighten 常表示给人解释信息、提升理解，语气比 tell 更正式。

usage_groups.group_title: 让人更清楚地理解某事
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 提供信息、解释或启发。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 教育、沟通、正式询问和思想讨论中常见。

examples.sentence: Could you enlighten me about the reasons for the decision?
examples.translation: 你能向我说明这个决定的原因吗？
examples.teaching_note: enlighten me about/on 是礼貌正式问法。

examples.sentence: The lecture enlightened students about the history of the region.
examples.translation: 这场讲座让学生更了解该地区的历史。
examples.teaching_note: 强调知识和理解增加。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The teacher explained the rule clearly.
The notice informed residents of the change.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: inform 只是提供信息，不一定带理解提升。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The example illuminated a difficult concept.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: illuminate 与 enlighten 很接近，强调使清楚。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The documentary was meant to edify its audience.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: edify 强调道德或知识上的提升。

antonyms.example_sentence: The unclear chart confused readers.
The advertisement misled consumers.

word_family.example_sentence: The book brought him a sense of enlightenment.
An enlightened policy considers long-term effects.

related_words.example_sentence: A small lamp filled the room with light.
related_words.difference_note: light 的“照亮”画面帮助记忆 enlighten：让人看得更清楚、理解得更明白。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. A; 使相符; 使达成一致 cause to coexist in harmony; make or show to be compatible: You may have to adjust your ideal to reconcile it with reality. 你得让你的理想符合现实 。 1 Since the couple could not reconcile their differences, they decided to get a divorce. 这对夫妻因为歧见不能调和决定离婚

headline.core_en: to make conflicting ideas, facts, or people compatible or friendly again

familiar_bridge.summary: reconcile 的核心是“让冲突的东西重新合得上”。

core.main: reconcile 可用于人和解，也可用于观点、理想与现实、数据之间相互一致。

usage_groups.group_title: 调和冲突的观点、事实或要求
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 让看似矛盾的东西能够共存。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 论证、科学解释、政策和个人选择中常见。

examples.sentence: You may need to reconcile your ideals with reality.
examples.translation: 你可能需要让理想与现实相协调。

examples.sentence: The new theory reconciles the conflicting results of earlier experiments.
examples.translation: 这个新理论调和了早期实验中相互矛盾的结果。
examples.teaching_note: 科学/论证语境常见。

usage_groups.group_title: 使人或群体和解
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 让关系恢复友好或至少不再敌对。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 家庭、政治、社会冲突中常见。

examples.sentence: The mediator tried to reconcile the two sides after months of hostility.
examples.translation: 几个月敌对之后，调解人试图让双方和解。
examples.teaching_note: 用于人与群体关系。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The design harmonizes modern materials with traditional forms.
The two sides settled the dispute.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The theory accommodates several exceptions.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: accommodate 强调把差异纳入。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: A neutral official mediated the dispute.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: mediate 是帮助双方和解。

antonyms.example_sentence: The policy alienated former supporters.
The two accounts conflict with each other.

word_family.example_sentence: Reconciliation between the communities took years.
The leader tried to conciliate angry voters.

related_words.example_sentence: Conciliate is the useful modern relative; concile itself is rare.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 博学者; 知识面广的人 a person of wide-ranging knowledge or learning: He is a brilliant polymath, (eB& Hig.

headline.core_en: a person with wide-ranging knowledge in many fields

familiar_bridge.summary: poly- 有“多”的意思；polymath 指知识面横跨许多领域的人。

core.main: polymath 强调知识面广、跨领域能力强，不只是成绩好。

usage_groups.group_title: 在许多领域都有深厚知识的人
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调跨学科、广泛学习和综合能力。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 人物介绍、历史、科学和文化评论中常见。

examples.sentence: Leonardo da Vinci is often described as a Renaissance polymath.
examples.translation: 列奥纳多·达·芬奇常被称为文艺复兴时期的通才。
examples.teaching_note: Renaissance polymath 是典型表达。

examples.sentence: The committee needed a polymath who could connect law, technology, and ethics.
examples.translation: 委员会需要一位能连接法律、技术和伦理的通才。
examples.teaching_note: 强调跨领域整合能力。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The team hired an expert in climate policy.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: expert 通常专于一域，polymath 广于多域。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The scholar studied ancient languages.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He was a true Renaissance man: scientist, artist, and engineer.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调跨领域才能。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: A generalist can see connections across fields.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: generalist 强调广泛而非专精。

antonyms.example_sentence: The hospital consulted a specialist in rare diseases.
A novice lacks experience in the field.

word_family.example_sentence: His polymathic curiosity crossed disciplinary boundaries.
word_family.difference_note: 形容词形式，用来形容跨领域的兴趣或能力。

word_family.example_sentence: Her polymathy allowed her to connect art, science, and politics.
word_family.difference_note: 名词形式，较少见，只作词族识别。

related_words.example_sentence: The polyglot translated among five languages.
related_words.difference_note: 同样用 poly- 表示“多”，这里是“多种语言”。

related_words.example_sentence: A triangle is the simplest polygon.
related_words.difference_note: poly- 在这里表示“多”，帮助迁移到 polymath 的“多领域”。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. fit; 发扬; 提倡 the act of furthering the growth or development of something: the promotion of friendship 促进友谊 | the promotion of AIDS awareness 促进对艾滋病的认识 n. 4+; 升职 the action of raising someone to a higher position or rank or the fact of being so raised: a promotion to divisional sales director 晋升为区域销售总监

headline.core_en: the act of encouraging growth or awareness; also advancement to a higher position

familiar_bridge.summary: promotion 一边是“往前推动”，一边是职位“往上升”。

core.main: promotion 常用于推广某事、促进发展，也用于工作升职。

usage_groups.group_title: 促进、推广或提高认知
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 让某事发展、被了解或被支持。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 公共卫生、教育、营销和政策语境中常见。

examples.sentence: The campaign focused on the promotion of AIDS awareness.
examples.translation: 这场活动重点是提升人们对艾滋病的认识。
examples.teaching_note: promotion of awareness 是原书义项的自然表达。

examples.sentence: The museum used social media for the promotion of its new exhibit.
examples.translation: 博物馆用社交媒体推广新展览。
examples.teaching_note: 也可用于推广活动。

usage_groups.group_title: 职位或等级提升
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 从较低职位升到更高职位。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 工作、组织和人事语境中常见。

examples.sentence: She received a promotion to regional sales director.
examples.translation: 她晋升为区域销售总监。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The job offers opportunities for advancement.
Advertising helped the product reach new buyers.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: 推广义相关，但更偏广告。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The grant supported the furtherance of medical research.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 正式表达，强调推进。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His elevation to chairman surprised many.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 更正式，强调升到更高位置。

antonyms.example_sentence: The scandal led to his demotion.
The suppression of information harmed public trust.

word_family.example_sentence: The policy promotes cooperation.
The company released promotional videos.

related_words.example_sentence: Promotion carries the idea of moving forward or upward.

source.raw_ocr_text: #44 vt， 抽离; 剥离; 提取 extract or remove (something): Data for the study was abstracted from hospital records. 研究的数据摘自医院记录 。 | abstract more water from streams 从蒸汽中提取更多的水分近义词 divorce

headline.core_en: to extract or remove; also existing as an idea rather than a concrete thing

familiar_bridge.summary: abstract 可以是“抽离出来”，也可以是“抽象的”；两个义项都和从具体事物中抽出有关。

core.main: 本词原书重点是动词“提取、抽离”，但 GRE 中形容词“抽象的”也很常见。

usage_groups.group_title: 从资料、记录或来源中提取信息
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 把需要的内容抽取出来。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 研究、数据、记录和正式写作中常见。

examples.sentence: Data for the study were abstracted from hospital records.
examples.translation: 这项研究的数据摘自医院记录。
examples.teaching_note: abstract data from records 是原书义项。

examples.sentence: The researchers abstracted key details from hundreds of interviews.
examples.translation: 研究者从数百次访谈中提取关键细节。
examples.teaching_note: 动词义强调提取。

usage_groups.group_title: 脱离具体事物、偏抽象
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 表示概念层面，而非具体可见物。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 哲学、艺术、学术写作中常见。

examples.sentence: The debate became too abstract for readers who wanted concrete examples.
examples.translation: 这场讨论对想要具体例子的读者来说变得过于抽象。
examples.teaching_note: 形容词义在阅读中常见。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The team extracted DNA from the sample.
The model remained theoretical.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The essay distills a complex debate into three claims.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: distill 强调提炼精华。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The project began with a conceptual framework.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 形容词义接近。

antonyms.example_sentence: The teacher gave concrete examples.
The editor inserted a new paragraph.

word_family.example_sentence: Mathematics often works through abstraction.
He looked abstracted during the meeting.

related_words.example_sentence: Both words can describe taking something out of a source.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. 抽象概念; 抽象名称 something that exists only as an idea: Whiteness, courage, and length are abstractions. 白 、 勇气和长度都是抽象概念 。 近义词 generality

headline.core_en: an idea considered apart from concrete things; the process of abstracting

familiar_bridge.summary: abstraction 是从具体事物中抽出共同概念，比如“白”“勇气”“长度”。

core.main: abstraction 可指一个抽象概念，也可指把具体内容抽象化的过程。

usage_groups.group_title: 只存在于思想中的抽象概念
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不是具体物体，而是概念或性质。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 哲学、数学、语言和学术讨论中常见。

examples.sentence: Whiteness, courage, and length are abstractions rather than physical objects.
examples.translation: 白、勇气和长度都是抽象概念，而不是具体物体。
examples.teaching_note: 原书例子说明 abstraction 的核心。

examples.sentence: The theory operates at a high level of abstraction.
examples.translation: 这个理论处在很高的抽象层次上。
examples.teaching_note: level of abstraction 是学术常见表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Justice is an idea, not an object.
The concept of freedom is difficult to define.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The statement has the weakness of excessive generality.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: generality 与抽象概括相关。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Intelligence is often treated as a psychological construct.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: construct 指理论中构造出的概念。

antonyms.example_sentence: A table is a concrete thing, not an abstraction.
The argument moves from particulars to abstractions.

word_family.example_sentence: The essay is too abstract for beginners.
The issue was discussed abstractly.

related_words.example_sentence: Students learn to generalize from examples.

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.
