source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 雄辩的; 言辞流利的; 有说服力的 fuent or persuasive in speaking or writing: an eloquent speec 户雄辩的讲演 | an eloquent spokesman 能言善辩的发言人派生 eloquence /'elokwons/ n. 雄辩 fluent or persuasive speaking or writing: She spoke with eloquence. 她的发言令人信服

headline.core_cn: 雄辩的；有说服力的；表达有力的
headline.core_en: fluent, forceful, and persuasive in speech, writing, or expression

familiar_bridge.summary: eloquent 不只是会说话，而是表达清楚、有力量、能打动人。

core.main: eloquent 可形容演讲者、文字，也可形容沉默、证据等“很有说明力”。

usage_groups.group_title: 讲话或文字流畅有力、能说服人
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调表达优美、有力、动人。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 演讲、写作、人物评价和评论中常见。

examples.sentence: Her eloquent speech persuaded many undecided voters.
examples.translation: 她雄辩的演讲说服了许多尚未决定的选民。
examples.teaching_note: eloquent speech 是原书义项。

examples.sentence: The essay is eloquent without becoming sentimental.
examples.translation: 这篇文章表达有力，却不流于煽情。
examples.teaching_note: 可用于文字风格评价。

usage_groups.group_title: 证据或沉默本身很有说明力
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 即使没有长篇话语，也能强烈表达某种意思。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 正式写作和评论中常见。

examples.sentence: The empty streets were eloquent evidence of the city’s fear.
examples.translation: 空荡的街道有力地说明了这座城市的恐惧。
examples.teaching_note: eloquent evidence 表示“很有说明力”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The argument was persuasive.
She is fluent in Spanish.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: fluent 强调流畅，不一定有感染力。

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 4}; 划分 divide into sections or categories: We were served a meal with each dish compartmentalized on a plastic tray. RA 1" HAAG RE—-T BREE. | Information is highly compartmentalized, and one knows little more than he needs to know. 情报分得很细 ， 各人除了他必须知道的之外其他的几乎一无所知

headline.core_cn: 分隔；分类；把……划分成部分
headline.core_en: to divide something into separate sections or categories

familiar_bridge.summary: compartment 是“隔间”；compartmentalize 就是把东西分到不同格子里。

core.main: compartmentalize 可指实物分隔，也常指信息、工作或情绪被分开处理。

usage_groups.group_title: 把信息、任务或空间分成独立部分
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调分区、分类、隔离。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 组织管理、情报、心理、学习和日常分类中常见。

examples.sentence: The agency compartmentalized information so that each team knew only what it needed.
examples.translation: 该机构把情报分隔开来，使每个小组只知道自己需要知道的部分。
examples.teaching_note: 原书义项的信息分隔非常典型。

examples.sentence: She learned to compartmentalize work stress and family life.
examples.translation: 她学会把工作压力和家庭生活分开处理。
examples.teaching_note: 心理/生活语境常见。

usage_groups.group_title: 把实物分隔放置
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 把不同东西放在不同格子或区域里。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 托盘、容器、空间设计中可见。

examples.sentence: The lunch tray compartmentalized each dish neatly.
examples.translation: 午餐托盘把每道菜整齐地分隔开。
examples.teaching_note: 具体实物义帮助理解抽象义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The teacher divided the class into groups.
The wall separates the two rooms.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The data were categorized by region.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: categorize 强调按类别划分。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The system segregated sensitive files from public ones.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: segregate 语气更强。

antonyms.example_sentence: The program integrates several databases.
The two departments merged last year.

word_family.example_sentence: Each compartment held a different tool.
Compartmentalization protected sensitive information.

related_words.example_sentence: The room was partitioned into offices.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. (84%; 僵局; 停顿 a situation, typically one involving opposing parties, in which no progress can be made: The union and management reached a deadlock over fringe benefits. 劳资双方在附加福利问题上陷入僵局

headline.core_en: a situation in which opposing sides cannot make progress

familiar_bridge.summary: deadlock 像“锁死了”：双方僵住，谁也推不动。

core.main: deadlock 常用于谈判、政治、劳资关系或投票中没有进展的局面。

usage_groups.group_title: 双方僵持导致无法推进
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调互相对立、进展停止。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 谈判、政治、劳资争议和组织决策中常见。

examples.sentence: The union and management reached a deadlock over benefits.

examples.sentence: A compromise was needed to break the deadlock in parliament.

examples.teaching_note: break the deadlock 表示打破僵局。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The talks reached an impasse.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Production came to a standstill.

antonyms.example_sentence: The talks produced a breakthrough.
The agreement ended the strike.

word_family.example_sentence: The image is of a situation locked in place.
The committee remained deadlocked.

headline.core_en: an object kept as a reminder of a place, event, or experience

familiar_bridge.summary: souvenir 是把经历带回来的“小证据”：看到它就想起某次旅行或事件。

core.main: souvenir 常指旅行、活动、地点或人生经历留下的纪念品。

usage_groups.group_title: 用来记住某个地方、事件或经历的小物件
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调物品的纪念功能。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He gave her a book as a token of thanks.

headline.core_cn: 放置；布置；安置；岗位安排
headline.core_en: the act of putting someone or something in a particular place or position

familiar_bridge.summary: place 是“放置、位置”；placement 就是放在哪里、怎样安排。

core.main: placement 可指家具/物品的摆放，也可指人员、学生或广告的位置安排。

usage_groups.group_title: 物品、文字或元素的放置位置
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调放在哪里、布局如何。

word_family.example_sentence: Floods displaced many families.

related_words.example_sentence: The position of the sign matters.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.i.&v.t. 翻转; 倒转 turn over or cause to turn over with a sudden sharp movement: He flipped to a fresh page and began to write. 他翻到另外一页 ， 开始写了起来 。 | flipped the tape to play the other side 把磁带翻过来 ， 播放另外一面

headline.core_cn: 翻转；快速翻动；突然转变
headline.core_en: to turn over quickly; to move through pages; or to change suddenly

familiar_bridge.summary: flip 的动作很快：啪地翻过去、翻页，或态度突然反转。

core.main: flip 可指实物翻面，也可指翻页、切换，甚至态度或局势突然改变。

usage_groups.group_title: 快速翻面或翻转
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调短促、突然的翻转动作。

examples.teaching_note: 原书义项，强调翻面。

examples.sentence: She flipped a coin to decide who would go first.
examples.translation: 她抛硬币决定谁先来。
examples.teaching_note: flip a coin 是固定动作。

usage_groups.group_title: 快速翻页或切换
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 在书页、频道、选项之间快速移动。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 阅读、屏幕、频道和日常动作中常见。

examples.teaching_note: flip to a page 是原书义项。

usage_groups.group_title: 态度、立场或局势突然转变
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 从一种状态迅速变成相反或不同状态。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 政治、市场、情绪和评论中常见。

examples.sentence: Several voters flipped from support to opposition after the debate.

examples.teaching_note: 抽象义表示立场转变。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Turn over the card to see the answer.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The image was inverted on the screen.

headline.core_cn: 公开撤回；放弃原先信念或主张
headline.core_en: to formally withdraw or say one no longer holds a belief, statement, or claim

familiar_bridge.summary: recant 常用于信仰、主张、证词：原来坚持，现在公开说不再坚持。

core.main: recant 的重点是正式撤回或放弃之前的观点，常在压力下发生。

usage_groups.group_title: 撤回原先的信念、主张或证词
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 公开承认自己不再持有某种观点。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 宗教、政治、法律和思想争论中常见。

examples.sentence: The scholar refused to recant his views under political pressure.
examples.translation: 这位学者拒绝在政治压力下撤回自己的观点。
examples.teaching_note: recant one’s views 是原书义项。

examples.sentence: The witness later recanted his testimony.
examples.translation: 这名证人后来撤回了证词。
examples.teaching_note: 法律语境常见。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The company withdrew its complaint.
He took back the accusation.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She repudiated the doctrine she once defended.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: repudiate 更强，表示拒绝接受。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He renounced his former allegiance.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: renounce 强调公开放弃。

antonyms.example_sentence: The witness affirmed his original statement.
She maintained her innocence.

word_family.example_sentence: The recantation came after months of pressure.
Modern cant is not a useful synonym, but it helps notice the spelling.

related_words.example_sentence: The leader renounced violence.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t， 使黯然失色; 使显得不重要或不突出; 超过 appear much more prominent or important than: Overshadowing all these problems was the shortage of food. 食物的短缺使所有这些问题都显得无足轻重 。 | What overshadows everything else is the castle. 城堡使其他一切都相形 Wah. i X44) obscure, eclipse

headline.core_cn: 使黯然失色；使显得不重要
headline.core_en: to make someone or something seem less important, noticeable, or impressive

familiar_bridge.summary: shadow 是“影子”；overshadow 就像一个更大的影子盖过去，让别的东西不显眼。

core.main: overshadow 常表示一个问题、人物或事件太突出，以至于其他东西被压下去。

usage_groups.group_title: 更大的问题或成就压过其他事物
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 让其他东西显得不重要、不突出。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 新闻、历史、人物评价和评论中常见。

examples.sentence: The shortage of food overshadowed all the other problems.
examples.translation: 食物短缺使所有其他问题都显得不那么重要。
examples.teaching_note: 原书义项：一个问题压过其他问题。

examples.sentence: Her later scandal overshadowed her early achievements.
examples.translation: 她后来的丑闻使她早期成就黯然失色。
examples.teaching_note: be overshadowed by 常用于人物评价。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The young actor outshone the star.
The error diminished the value of the report.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The new discovery eclipsed earlier findings.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: eclipse 与 overshadow 很接近。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Clouds obscured the mountain.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: obscure 强调遮蔽或使难见。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report highlighted the main risk.
The lighting enhanced the painting’s colors.

word_family.example_sentence: A larger shadow can cover what is behind it.
His role was overshadowed by the leader’s fame.

related_words.example_sentence: The moon can eclipse the sun.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. (思想 、 事件等的 ) 背景; 环境 the circumstances that form the setting for an event, statement, or idea, and in terms of which it can be fully understood and assessed: assess sb. in the context of his times 联系人物的时代背景来评价某人 | It is in such a context that the military took over. 军方就是在这种情况下接管的 。 n. 上下文; 语境 the parts of something written or spoken that immediately precede and follow a word or passage and clarify its meaning: You should be able to guess the meaning of the word from the context. 你从上下文应该能猜出单词的意思

headline.core_en: the situation, circumstances, or surrounding text that help explain meaning

familiar_bridge.summary: context 就是“放在周围环境里看”：事件有时代背景，单词有上下文。

core.main: context 有两个常见义项：思想/事件的背景，以及词句前后的上下文。

usage_groups.group_title: 事件、思想或行为的背景环境
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 帮助解释某事为什么发生、如何评价。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 历史、社会科学、阅读分析和评论中常见。

examples.sentence: The leader must be assessed in the context of his times.
examples.translation: 评价这位领导人必须联系他的时代背景。
examples.teaching_note: in the context of 是原书义项。

examples.sentence: It was in this context that the military took power.
examples.translation: 正是在这种背景下，军方接管了政权。
examples.teaching_note: context 可解释事件发生条件。

usage_groups.group_title: 词语或句子的上下文
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 前后文字帮助判断含义。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 语言学习、阅读理解和翻译中常见。

examples.sentence: You can often guess a word’s meaning from the context.
examples.translation: 你常常可以根据上下文猜出单词意思。
examples.teaching_note: from the context 是阅读中常用。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The background explains the conflict.
The setting of the novel is a small town.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The decision made sense under the circumstances.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: circumstances 强调具体条件。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The theory provides a framework for analysis.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: framework 更抽象，偏分析框架。

antonyms.example_sentence: The quote is misleading in isolation.
antonyms.difference_note: 与放在 context 中相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Decontextualization can distort meaning.

word_family.example_sentence: Contextual clues help readers infer meaning.
The historian contextualized the event.

related_words.example_sentence: Context is the text around a word or idea.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 障碍、 困难等 ) 不可和逾越的: 难以克服的 (of difficulties, problems, etc.) that cannot be dealt with successfully: an insurmountable obstacle 7 ¥] aki th FRAG | struggle against insurmountable difficulties 同种种难以克服的困难作斗争

headline.core_cn: 无法克服的；难以逾越的
headline.core_en: too great or difficult to overcome

familiar_bridge.summary: surmount 是“越过、克服”；insurmountable 就是难到越不过去。

core.main: insurmountable 常形容困难、障碍、问题大到无法解决。

usage_groups.group_title: 困难或障碍大到无法克服
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调几乎没有办法越过或解决。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 论证、政策、个人困境和正式评论中常见。

examples.sentence: The lack of funding created an insurmountable obstacle for the project.
examples.translation: 资金不足给项目造成了无法克服的障碍。

examples.sentence: At first the difficulties seemed insurmountable, but the team found a way forward.
examples.translation: 起初这些困难似乎无法克服，但团队找到了前进办法。
examples.teaching_note: seem insurmountable 常见。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The task seemed impossible at first.
The evidence against him was overwhelming.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: overwhelming 强调强度大到压倒。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The mountain seemed unconquerable to early climbers.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 强调无法战胜。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The conflict became intractable.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: intractable 指问题难处理、难解决。

antonyms.example_sentence: The obstacle was difficult but surmountable.
The problem became manageable with more time.

word_family.example_sentence: They surmounted many difficulties.
The challenge was surmountable with help.

related_words.example_sentence: The image is of climbing over a mountain-like obstacle.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 琐碎的; 没有意义的; 没有价值的 not having any serious purpose or value: a frivolous activity 没有价值的活动

headline.core_en: not serious, useful, or worth much attention

familiar_bridge.summary: frivolous 常批评某事“不严肃、不值得认真对待”。

core.main: frivolous 可形容活动、开支、理由、诉讼或人态度轻浮无价值。

usage_groups.group_title: 事情轻浮、琐碎或没有实际价值
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 缺少严肃目的或重要性。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 评价活动、消费、诉讼、理由和行为时常见。

examples.sentence: The committee refused to waste money on frivolous projects.
examples.translation: 委员会拒绝把钱浪费在无意义的项目上。
examples.teaching_note: frivolous projects 表示不值得投入。

examples.sentence: The judge dismissed the case as frivolous.
examples.translation: 法官认为该案毫无价值，予以驳回。
examples.teaching_note: 法律语境中 frivolous lawsuit/case 很常见。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The excuse sounded silly.
The argument focused on trivial details.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The article offered a superficial analysis.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: superficial 强调停留表面。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His facetious remark annoyed the audience.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: facetious 指不合时宜地开玩笑。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report raised serious concerns.
The proposal made a substantial contribution.

word_family.example_sentence: The ceremony mixed dignity with moments of frivolity.
The complaint was filed frivolously.

related_words.example_sentence: Trivial details can distract from serious issues.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 可互换的; 等同的 mutually interchangeable: Fruits and vegetables are regarded as fungible in this diet. 水果和蔬菜在这份食谱中是可以互相替代的 。 i 034] interchangeable

headline.core_en: able to be exchanged or replaced by another identical or equivalent item

familiar_bridge.summary: fungible 常说钱、商品、单位资源可以彼此替代；不是所有“相似”都叫 fungible。

core.main: fungible 强调每一单位没有独特性，可以等值替换。

usage_groups.group_title: 同类单位可等值互换
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调个体之间没有重要差别，可以彼此替代。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 经济、法律、资源、金融和抽象讨论中常见。

examples.sentence: Money is largely fungible: one dollar can replace another dollar.
examples.translation: 钱在很大程度上是可互换的：一美元可以替代另一美元。
examples.teaching_note: money is fungible 是最直观理解。

examples.sentence: The policy treated different sources of funding as fungible resources.
examples.translation: 该政策把不同来源的资金视为可互换资源。
examples.teaching_note: 资源/资金语境常见。

usage_groups.group_title: 某些类别在特定制度中被视为可替代
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 现实中不一定完全相同，但在某规则下被当作等价。
usage_groups.when_to_use: 政策、饮食、分类和制度分析中常见。

examples.sentence: In this diet, fruits and vegetables are treated as partly fungible categories.
examples.translation: 在这份食谱中，水果和蔬菜被视为部分可互相替代的类别。
examples.teaching_note: 接近原书例句，强调规则下的替代。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The two parts are interchangeable.
The battery is replaceable.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: replaceable 只表示能被替换，不一定等值。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The two methods produced equivalent results.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: equivalent 强调价值或效果相等。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The ingredients are substitutable in this recipe.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 更技术化，强调可替换。

antonyms.example_sentence: The artifact is unique and cannot be replaced.
Her knowledge of the archive is irreplaceable.

word_family.example_sentence: The fungibility of money complicates the accounting.

related_words.example_sentence: The parts can interchange without changing the machine.
